title: Dryopteris labordei
intro: Dryopteris labordei, also known as Laborde's wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of D. labordei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from D. labordei include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Dryopteris labordei is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which consists of over 200 species of ferns. The genus Dryopteris is part of the order Polypodiales, which includes over 9,000 species of ferns. The order Polypodiales is further classified under the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants known as leptosporangiate ferns. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the Dryopteris labordei and its related species within the fern family.
